reasoning: 8x zoom inspection: (1) Crystal wings show multi-tone blue gradient transitions rather than strict 2-3 hard-stop pixel art shading — at least 4-5 distinct blue values create a smooth-ish fade that reads as AI-generated gradient rather than deliberate dithering. (2) Outline width is inconsistent: bottom/base of character has heavier dark border (~2px equivalent) while upper wing extremities taper to 1px or missing outline on some tips. (3) Minor color bleed visible at wing-body junction where blue pixels encroach into the white body region by 1-2 pixels in an irregular pattern. (4) Background solid dark area is clean with no noise. (5) Symmetry is approximate but not perfect — left crystal cluster has one extra pixel row vs right. (6) No sub-pixel rendering artifacts; pixel grid is clean and consistent at 1px resolution. (7) Semantic integrity is solid — reads clearly as an active echo-box sprite character on a glowing pedestal. (8) No pattern tiling artifacts. Overall: passes basic pixel art grid discipline but fails on gradient shading and minor outline inconsistency, placing it in marginal territory.
